rulings.Case Title: LETICIA SANCHEZ ROMERO VS. LUIS SANCHEZ ROCHA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Cancellation Of Sale Contract, For Forfeiture And Release Of Deposit, And For An Order For Defendant To Vacate Property
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 6, 2026. Line 1. OTHER AMY HARRINGTON (COURT APPOINTED REFEREE PER MAR-06-25 ORDER) MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Cancellation Of Sale Contract, For Forfeiture And Release Of Deposit, And For An Order For Defendant To Vacate Property is DENIED without prejudice. Motion is not supported by any evidence. Declaration submitted with reply is untimely. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JUN ZHANG VS. MARTIN LEE ENG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moiton To Vacate Order Denying Defendant'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 6, 2026. Line 7. DEFENDANT MARTIN ENG Notice Of Motion And Moiton To Vacate Order Denying Defendant'S Motion For Summary Judgment is DENIED. The Court denied Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment on the basis that Defendant had not met his initial burden of showing that Plaintiff could not establish the elements of his cause of action, i.e., that Defendant had not shown that Plaintiff did not possess and could not reasonably obtain the evidence necessary to establish the essential elements of his cause of action. The Court did not apply an incorrect legal standard. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JUN ZHANG VS. MARTIN LEE ENG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 6, 2026. Line 7. DEFENDANT DAVID GARDUNO DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer from notice of entry of order. The Fourth Amended Complaint states a cause of action on its face. Plaintiff's requests for affirmative relief, made in opposition to this demurrer only and not on proper notice in accordance with CCP 1005 or other applicable authorizing statutes, are denied as procedurally improper. The Court makes no determination as to the propriety of Defendant Garduno's Prejudgment Claim of Right to Possession. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
